title: Authentication middleware accepts alg:none JWT tokens
description: The JWT verification call does not pass an algorithms whitelist; jws.verify() therefore accepts tokens signed with alg:none, allowing forgery without the signing key.

title: Hard-coded RSA private key in source tree
description: A full RSA private key is checked into the repository and used by the JWT signer. Anyone with read access to the repo can forge tokens.
suggested_fix: Load the private key from a secret manager; omit the file from the repo; rotate after disclosure.
attack_scenario: Attacker clones the repo, signs a token with role=admin, obtains full administrative access.
